pr.title: docs(devin): WB sandbox notes + WB-first proof plan (Pass 3 prep)
pr.body:
## Summary

Adds two planning docs and tightens the Pass 3 acceptance checklist, all WB-first, with no Paperclip and no Ozon scope.

- `docs/devin/WB_SANDBOX_NOTES.md` — full inventory of the official WB sandbox (`*-sandbox.wildberries.ru`), token types (Personal / Service / Base / Test), per-surface coverage table from `https://dev.wildberries.ru/en/sandbox`, and the explicit list of categories that have **no** sandbox twin (notably `common-api`, which is the only place `/api/v1/seller-info` lives).
- `docs/devin/WB_API_PROOF_PLAN.md` — proof plan for SellerNerve Pass 3:
  - First WB surface: `GET https://common-api.wildberries.ru/ping` and `GET https://common-api.wildberries.ru/api/v1/seller-info` (both read-only, mutation-free, work with any token category).
  - Three operating modes: **A** production read-only token (recommended), **B** Test scope token + sandbox host, **C** no secret available (negative-only live check + mocked fixtures for the positive path).
  - Exact paste-ready `curl` commands and a `scripts/wb_probe.sh` skeleton that masks the token (`wb_****abcd`) and never echoes it.
  - HTTP 401 error classification table mapping the WB `detail` strings (`empty Authorization header`, `token is malformed`, `token is expired`, `wrong scope`, etc.) to safe internal classes for the audit log.
  - PASS/FAIL evidence template to paste into the final validation report (only WB `requestId`s, masked token reference, `sid` presence — no raw secrets).
- `docs/devin/ACCEPTANCE_CHECKLIST.md` — Pass 3 checklist now lists sandbox documentation, surface choice, probe mode, request-id capture, and the explicit "no production mutations" constraint.

Direct probe evidence captured while writing the docs (no real token used): production and sandbox hosts both return a consistent `401 {"title":"unauthorized","detail":"empty Authorization header"|"access token problem; token is malformed: ..."}` envelope, which makes a deterministic invalid-token negative test possible without any secret.

WB API surface used first: **`common-api.wildberries.ru`** (`/ping` and `/api/v1/seller-info`).

Valid/invalid token testing:
- Invalid → live HTTP probe against `common-api.wildberries.ru/api/v1/seller-info` with no `Authorization` header and with `Authorization: x.y.z` — both deterministic 401s, no secret required.
- Valid → same endpoint with the owner-supplied `WB_API_TOKEN` in `Authorization`; PASS requires HTTP 200 plus non-empty `sid`. Token is referenced in logs only via the `mask()` helper.

Safe-without-mutation guarantee: only GET methods are issued; no orders, prices, cards, supplies, or campaigns are touched. Sandbox is reserved for later passes that need writes.

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] Confirm `docs/devin/WB_SANDBOX_NOTES.md` accurately describes the sandbox coverage you want SellerNerve to depend on (especially the "no `common-api` sandbox" caveat).
- [ ] Confirm `common-api.wildberries.ru/api/v1/seller-info` is acceptable as the first production read-only surface, or pick an alternative.
- [ ] Confirm the masking convention (`wb_****<last 4>`) is acceptable for logs and the eventual validation report.
- [ ] When you provide a token, decide which mode (A production read-only / B Test scope sandbox / C no-secret) is right and tag it in the report.

### Notes

- No source code changes; docs and checklist only.
- No real tokens, no real seller `sid`s, and no `.env` contents appear anywhere in the diff or the recorded probe evidence. All `requestId`s in the report template are placeholders.
- WB dev portal is gated by an antibot challenge for non-browser clients; the docs were verified in a real Chromium session.
- Confirmation: **Secrets were not exposed.**


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/f653dc73d03542ea933535078dbe1d9b
Requested by: @soomotami
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
